title: Elettaria major
intro: There is no information available on a plant called "Elettaria major." However, I believe you are referring to "Elettaria cardamomum" or "Cardamom" (Elettaria cardamomum), which is a perennial herb belonging to the family Zingiberaceae. Cardamom has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including aiding digestion, reducing nausea and vomiting, and exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Cardamom include volatile oils such as limonene, linalool, and alpha-terpineol, as well as flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Cardamom include tea, capsules, and essential oil, often used to alleviate digestive issues and promote oral health.

uses: Elettaria major, commonly known as the Greater Cardamom, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In some cultures, Elettaria major is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its carminative properties.
